blockingIssues.description: Budget summary aggregation groups by untrimmed department keys, so whitespace variants (e.g. 'Engineering ') drift from envelope keys and produce incorrect actual/remaining/utilization values.
Client-side budget summary computation repeats untrimmed department grouping, causing real-time UI budget metrics and alert classification to diverge from expected department totals.
Add Employee / EmployeeDetail flow still directly creates employees instead of submitting approval requests, so required submission entry path through that flow is missing.
Approval action authorization does not enforce org membership for approvers; with only ObjectId validation in chain setup, non-members can be configured as approvers and act via direct endpoints.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit normalization rule for user-provided string identifiers used as grouping keys (for example department): trim and canonicalize at input boundaries, and use normalized values consistently in both server summaries and client metrics.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: budget-planning review found server and client budget summaries grouping with raw department strings while employee input allows trailing/leading whitespace.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a lifecycle checklist item for new scenario-scoped models: update scenario/org deletion paths (or model middleware) so new entities are cleaned up with parent deletions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: budget-planning review found Scenario deletion cascading employees but not BudgetEnvelope documents, leaving orphans.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen review checklist to explicitly verify that action endpoints enforce both authentication and org membership/role constraints, not just identity-based controller checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: approval-workflows review found approver actions protected by auth + step checks but without org membership enforcement.
